explanation_template: HubSpot shows deal closed at {hubspot_amount} but QuickBooks invoice is {qbo_amount} ({pct}% of deal). This usually indicates a 30% deposit or partial payment recorded.

pattern_name: Closed-Won deal with no QuickBooks invoice
explanation_template: Deal '{deal_name}' closed in HubSpot on {close_date} for {amount} but no matching QuickBooks invoice was found. Common billing miss pattern.
'{hubspot_name}' and '{qbo_name}' appear to be the same company — only the legal-entity suffix differs.
Amounts match but currency is recorded differently: '{hubspot_currency}' vs '{qbo_currency}'. No actual conflict — formatting only.

pattern_name: Polish NIP in QBO, missing in HubSpot
explanation_template: QuickBooks has a Polish NIP ({nip}) for this customer; HubSpot does not. Adding the NIP to HubSpot will improve future EU invoice matching.

pattern_name: Multiple QBO invoices for one HubSpot deal
explanation_template: HubSpot deal {deal_name} ({hubspot_amount}) appears split across {invoice_count} QuickBooks invoices summing to {qbo_total}. Common for milestone billing or multi-product deals.
QuickBooks customer is recorded as a sub-customer ('{qbo_name}'). HubSpot stores the parent or child name only. Likely the same legal entity.

pattern_name: Date appears different but resolves identically
explanation_template: Dates are written differently ('{hubspot_date}' vs '{qbo_date}') but both resolve to {iso_date}. Format-only — no conflict.

pattern_name: HubSpot Closed-Won but QBO invoice still Open
explanation_template: HubSpot reports the deal Closed-Won but the QuickBooks invoice is still {qbo_status}. Cash hasn't landed; flag for AR follow-up.

pattern_name: HubSpot contact has no associated company
explanation_template: HubSpot deal '{deal_name}' has a contact ({email}) but no associated company. QuickBooks customer is '{qbo_name}'. Hard to fuzzy-match without the company field.
